-- Hoogle documentation, generated by Haddock
-- See Hoogle, http://www.haskell.org/hoogle/


-- | Package for development of simple and efficient tracers based on the
--   arrow based contra-tracer package
@package trace-dispatcher
@version 2.11.1

module Cardano.Logging.DocuGenerator.Result
data DocuResult
DocuTracer :: Builder -> DocuResult
DocuMetric :: Builder -> DocuResult
DocuDatapoint :: Builder -> DocuResult
unpackDocu :: DocuResult -> Builder
isTracer :: DocuResult -> Bool
isMetric :: DocuResult -> Bool
isDatapoint :: DocuResult -> Bool
instance GHC.Show.Show Cardano.Logging.DocuGenerator.Result.DocuResult

module Cardano.Logging.DocuGenerator.Tree

-- | Non-empty, possibly infinite, multi-way trees; also known as <i>rose
--   trees</i>.
data () => Tree a
Node :: a -> [Tree a] -> Tree a

-- | label value
[rootLabel] :: Tree a -> a

-- | zero or more child trees
[subForest] :: Tree a -> [Tree a]

-- | Fold a tree into a "summary" value in depth-first order.
--   
--   For each node in the tree, apply <tt>f</tt> to the <tt>rootLabel</tt>
--   and the result of applying <tt>f</tt> to each <tt>subForest</tt>.
--   
--   This is also known as the catamorphism on trees.
--   
--   <h4><b>Examples</b></h4>
--   
--   Sum the values in a tree:
--   
--   <pre>
--   foldTree (\x xs -&gt; sum (x:xs)) (Node 1 [Node 2 [], Node 3 []]) == 6
--   </pre>
--   
--   Find the maximum value in the tree:
--   
--   <pre>
--   foldTree (\x xs -&gt; maximum (x:xs)) (Node 1 [Node 2 [], Node 3 []]) == 3
--   </pre>
--   
--   Count the number of leaves in the tree:
--   
--   <pre>
--   foldTree (\_ xs -&gt; if null xs then 1 else sum xs) (Node 1 [Node 2 [], Node 3 []]) == 2
--   </pre>
--   
--   Find depth of the tree; i.e. the number of branches from the root of
--   the tree to the furthest leaf:
--   
--   <pre>
--   foldTree (\_ xs -&gt; if null xs then 0 else 1 + maximum xs) (Node 1 [Node 2 [], Node 3 []]) == 1
--   </pre>
--   
--   You can even implement traverse using foldTree:
--   
--   <pre>
--   traverse' f = foldTree (\x xs -&gt; liftA2 Node (f x) (sequenceA xs))
--   </pre>
foldTree :: (a -> [b] -> b) -> Tree a -> b
printTree :: Tree Text -> Text
printList :: (a -> Builder) -> [a] -> Builder
toForest :: [[Text]] -> Forest Text

module Cardano.Logging.Prometheus.Exposition
type MetricName = Text
renderExpositionFromSample :: Bool -> Sample -> Text
renderExpositionFromSampleWith :: [(MetricName, Builder)] -> Bool -> Sample -> Text

module Cardano.Logging.Types.NodeInfo

-- | NodeInfo
data NodeInfo
NodeInfo :: Text -> Text -> Text -> Text -> UTCTime -> UTCTime -> NodeInfo
[niName] :: NodeInfo -> Text
[niProtocol] :: NodeInfo -> Text
[niVersion] :: NodeInfo -> Text
[niCommit] :: NodeInfo -> Text
[niStartTime] :: NodeInfo -> UTCTime
[niSystemStartTime] :: NodeInfo -> UTCTime
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Cardano.Logging.Types.NodeInfo.NodeInfo
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Cardano.Logging.Types.NodeInfo.NodeInfo
instance Control.DeepSeq.NFData Cardano.Logging.Types.NodeInfo.NodeInfo
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Cardano.Logging.Types.NodeInfo.NodeInfo
instance GHC.Show.Show Cardano.Logging.Types.NodeInfo.NodeInfo
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Cardano.Logging.Types.NodeInfo.NodeInfo

module Cardano.Logging.Types.NodeStartupInfo

-- | NodeStartupInfo
--   
--   This information is taken from <tt>BasicInfoShelleyBased</tt>. It is
--   required for 'cardano-tracer' service (particularly, for RTView).
data NodeStartupInfo
NodeStartupInfo :: Text -> NominalDiffTime -> Word64 -> Word64 -> NodeStartupInfo
[suiEra] :: NodeStartupInfo -> Text
[suiSlotLength] :: NodeStartupInfo -> NominalDiffTime
[suiEpochLength] :: NodeStartupInfo -> Word64
[suiSlotsPerKESPeriod] :: NodeStartupInfo -> Word64
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Cardano.Logging.Types.NodeStartupInfo.NodeStartupInfo
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Cardano.Logging.Types.NodeStartupInfo.NodeStartupInfo
instance Control.DeepSeq.NFData Cardano.Logging.Types.NodeStartupInfo.NodeStartupInfo
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Cardano.Logging.Types.NodeStartupInfo.NodeStartupInfo
instance GHC.Show.Show Cardano.Logging.Types.NodeStartupInfo.NodeStartupInfo
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Cardano.Logging.Types.NodeStartupInfo.NodeStartupInfo

module Cardano.Logging.Utils
threadLabelMe :: String -> IO ()

-- | Run an IO action which may throw an exception in a loop. On exception,
--   the action will be re-run after a pause. That pause doubles which each
--   exception, but is reset when the action runs long enough.
runInLoop :: IO () -> (SomeException -> IO ()) -> Word -> Word -> IO ()

-- | Convenience function for a Show instance to be converted to text
--   immediately
showT :: Show a => a -> Text
showTReal :: RealFloat a => a -> Text
showTHex :: Integral a => a -> Text


-- | Run a TCP server, with hardening against connection flooding
module Cardano.Logging.Prometheus.NetworkRun

-- | Parameters specifying how the TCP server should be run
data NetworkRunParams
NetworkRunParams :: !Int -> !Int -> !Int -> !Double -> !Int -> !Int -> !String -> NetworkRunParams

-- | Release socket after inactivity (seconds)
[runSocketTimeout] :: NetworkRunParams -> !Int

-- | Graceful closing of socket (milliseconds), 0 to disable
[runSocketGraceful] :: NetworkRunParams -> !Int

-- | Close socket if more than (runRecvMaxSize - 1) bytes received; choose
--   a small power of 2
[runRecvMaxSize] :: NetworkRunParams -> !Int

-- | Limit requests per second (may be &lt; 0.0), 0.0 to disable
[runRateLimit] :: NetworkRunParams -> !Double

-- | Limit total number of incoming connections, 0 to disable
[runConnLimitGlobal] :: NetworkRunParams -> !Int

-- | Limit number of incoming connections from the same host, 0 to disable
[runConnLimitPerHost] :: NetworkRunParams -> !Int

-- | The server name - exclusively used for labeling GHC threads
[runServerName] :: NetworkRunParams -> !String
type TimeoutServer a = NetworkRunParams -> IO () -> Socket -> IO a
defaultRunParams :: String -> NetworkRunParams

-- | Returns an IO action that will run a TCP server conforming to the run
--   parameters. Will bind to localhost / loopback device only if no host
--   name is specified. Will throw an exception when TCP server startup
--   fails - the caller is responsible for appropriately reacting to that.
mkTCPServerRunner :: NetworkRunParams -> Maybe HostName -> PortNumber -> TimeoutServer () -> IO (IO ())


module Control.Tracer.Arrow

-- | Formal representation of a tracer arrow as a Kleisli arrow over some
--   monad, but tagged so that we know whether it has any effects which
--   will emit a trace.
data TracerA m a b

-- | An emitting part, followed by a non-emitting part. The non-emitting
--   part is there so that later emitting parts can be tacked-on later.
[Emitting] :: Kleisli m a x -> Kleisli m x b -> TracerA m a b

-- | No emitting. There may be side-effects, but they are assumed to be
--   benign and will be discarded by <a>runTracerA</a>.
[Squelching] :: Kleisli m a b -> TracerA m a b

-- | The resulting Kleisli arrow includes all of the effects required to do
--   the emitting part.
runTracerA :: Monad m => TracerA m a () -> Kleisli m a ()

-- | Pure computation in a tracer: no side effects or emits.
compute :: Applicative m => (a -> b) -> TracerA m a b

-- | Do an emitting effect. Contrast with <a>effect</a> which does not make
--   the tracer an emitting tracer.
emit :: Applicative m => (a -> m ()) -> TracerA m a ()

-- | Do a non-emitting effect. This effect will only be run if some part of
--   the tracer downstream emits (see <a>emit</a>).
effect :: (a -> m b) -> TracerA m a b

-- | Ignore the input and do not emit. The name is intended to lead to
--   clear and suggestive arrow expressions.
squelch :: Applicative m => TracerA m a ()

-- | Use a natural transformation to change the underlying monad.
nat :: (forall x. m x -> n x) -> TracerA m a b -> TracerA n a b
instance GHC.Base.Monad m => Control.Category.Category (Control.Tracer.Arrow.TracerA m)
instance GHC.Base.Monad m => Control.Arrow.Arrow (Control.Tracer.Arrow.TracerA m)
instance GHC.Base.Monad m => Control.Arrow.ArrowChoice (Control.Tracer.Arrow.TracerA m)


-- | <h3>General usage</h3>
--   
--   <a>Tracer</a> is a contravariant functor intended to express the
--   pattern in which values of its parameter type are used to produce
--   effects which are prescribed by the caller, as in tracing, logging,
--   code instrumentation, etc.
--   
--   Programs should be written to use as specific a tracer as possible,
--   i.e. to take as a parameter a <tt>Tracer m domainSpecificType</tt>. To
--   combine these programs into an executable which does meaningful
--   tracing, an implementation of that tracing should be used to make a
--   <tt>Tracer probablyIO implementationTracingType</tt>, which is
--   <a>contramap</a>ped to fit <tt>Tracer m domainSpecificType</tt>
--   wherever it is needed, for the various <tt>domainSpecificType</tt>s
--   that appear throughout the program.
--   
--   <h3>An example</h3>
--   
--   This short example shows how a tracer can be deployed, highlighting
--   the use of <a>contramap</a> to fit a general tracer which writes text
--   to a file, where a specific tracer which takes domain-specific events
--   is expected.
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- Writes text to some log file.
--   traceToLogFile :: FilePath -&gt; Tracer IO Text
--   
--   -- Domain-specific event type.
--   data Event = EventA | EventB Int
--   
--   -- The log-file format for an Event.
--   eventToText :: Event -&gt; Text
--   
--   -- Some action that can use any tracer on Event, in any monad.
--   actionWithTrace :: Monad m =&gt; Tracer m Event -&gt; m ()
--   actionWithTrace tracer = do
--     traceWith tracer EventA
--     traceWith tracer (EventB 42)
--   
--   -- Set up a log file tracer, then use it where the Event tracer is expected.
--   main :: IO ()
--   main = do
--     textTacer &lt;- traceToLogFile "log.txt"
--     let eventTracer :: Tracer IO Event
--         eventTracer = contramap eventToText tracer
--     actionWithTrace eventTracer
--   </pre>
module Control.Tracer

-- | This type describes some effect in <tt>m</tt> which depends upon some
--   value of type <tt>a</tt>, for which the <i>output value</i> is not of
--   interest (only the effects).
--   
--   The motivating use case is to describe tracing, logging, monitoring,
--   and similar features, in which the programmer wishes to provide some
--   values to some <i>other</i> program which will do some real world side
--   effect, such as writing to a log file or bumping a counter in some
--   monitoring system.
--   
--   The actual implementation of such a program will probably work on
--   rather large, domain-agnostic types like <tt>Text</tt>,
--   <tt>ByteString</tt>, JSON values for structured logs, etc.
--   
--   But the call sites which ultimately <i>invoke</i> these
--   implementations will deal with smaller, domain-specific types that
--   concisely describe events, metrics, debug information, etc.
--   
--   This difference is reconciled by the <a>Contravariant</a> instance for
--   <a>Tracer</a>. <a>contramap</a> is used to change the input type of a
--   tracer. This allows for a more general tracer to be used where a more
--   specific one is expected.
--   
--   Intuitively: if you can map your domain-specific type <tt>Event</tt>
--   to a <tt>Text</tt> representation, then any <tt>Tracer m Text</tt> can
--   stand in where a <tt>Tracer m Event</tt> is required.
--   
--   <pre>
--   eventToText :: Event -&gt; Text
--   
--   traceTextToLogFile :: Tracer m Text
--   
--   traceEventToLogFile :: Tracer m Event
--   traceEventToLogFile = contramap eventToText traceTextToLogFile
--   </pre>
--   
--   Effectful tracers that actually do interesting stuff can be defined
--   using <a>emit</a>, and composed via <a>contramap</a>.
--   
--   The <a>nullTracer</a> can be used as a stand-in for any tracer, doing
--   no side-effects and producing no interesting value.
--   
--   To deal with branching, the arrow interface on the underlying
--   <a>Tracer</a> should be used. Arrow notation can be helpful here.
--   
--   For example, a common pattern is to trace only some variants of a sum
--   type.
--   
--   <pre>
--   data Event = This Int | That Bool
--   
--   traceOnlyThat :: Tracer m Int -&gt; Tracer m Bool
--   traceOnlyThat tr = Tracer $ proc event -&gt; do
--     case event of
--       This i -&gt; use tr  -&lt; i
--       That _ -&gt; squelch -&lt; ()
--   </pre>
--   
--   The key point of using the arrow representation we have here is that
--   this tracer will not necessarily need to force <tt>event</tt>: if the
--   input tracer <tt>tr</tt> does not force its value, then <tt>event</tt>
--   will not be forced. To elaborate, suppose <tt>tr</tt> is
--   <tt>nullTracer</tt>. Then this expression becomes
--   
--   <pre>
--   classify (This i) = Left i
--   classify (That _) = Right ()
--   
--   traceOnlyThat tr
--   = Tracer $ Pure classify &gt;&gt;&gt; (squelch ||| squelch) &gt;&gt;&gt; Pure (either id id)
--   = Tracer $ Pure classify &gt;&gt;&gt; Pure (either (const (Left ())) (const (Right ()))) &gt;&gt;&gt; Pure (either id id)
--   = Tracer $ Pure (classify &gt;&gt;&gt; either (const (Left ())) (const (Right ())) &gt;&gt;&gt; either id id)
--   </pre>
--   
--   So that when this tracer is run by <a>traceWith</a> we get
--   
--   <pre>
--   traceWith (traceOnlyThat tr) x
--   = traceWith (Pure _)
--   = pure ()
--   </pre>
--   
--   It is _essential_ that the computation of the tracing effects cannot
--   itself have side-effects, as this would ruin the ability to
--   short-circuit when it is known that no tracing will be done: the
--   side-effects of a branch could change the outcome of another branch.
--   This would fly in the face of a crucial design goal: you can leave
--   your tracer calls in the program so they do not bitrot, but can also
--   make them zero runtime cost by substituting <a>nullTracer</a>
--   appropriately.
newtype Tracer m a
Tracer :: TracerA m a () -> Tracer m a
[runTracer] :: Tracer m a -> TracerA m a ()

-- | Run a tracer with a given input.
traceWith :: Monad m => Tracer m a -> a -> m ()

-- | Inverse of <a>use</a>.
arrow :: TracerA m a () -> Tracer m a

-- | Inverse of <a>arrow</a>. Useful when writing arrow tracers which use a
--   contravariant tracer (the newtype in this module).
use :: Tracer m a -> TracerA m a ()

-- | Ignore the input and do not emit. The name is intended to lead to
--   clear and suggestive arrow expressions.
squelch :: Applicative m => TracerA m a ()

-- | Do an emitting effect. Contrast with <a>effect</a> which does not make
--   the tracer an emitting tracer.
emit :: Applicative m => (a -> m ()) -> TracerA m a ()

-- | Do a non-emitting effect. This effect will only be run if some part of
--   the tracer downstream emits (see <a>emit</a>).
effect :: (a -> m b) -> TracerA m a b

-- | A tracer which does nothing.
nullTracer :: Monad m => Tracer m a

-- | Trace strings to stdout. Output could be jumbled when this is used
--   from multiple threads. Consider <a>debugTracer</a> instead.
stdoutTracer :: Tracer IO String

-- | Trace strings using <a>traceM</a>. This will use stderr. See
--   documentation in <a>Debug.Trace</a> for more details.
debugTracer :: Applicative m => Tracer m String

-- | Use a natural transformation to change the <tt>m</tt> type. This is
--   useful, for instance, to use concrete IO tracers in monad transformer
--   stacks that have IO as their base.
natTracer :: forall m n s. (forall x. m x -> n x) -> Tracer m s -> Tracer n s

-- | Use a natural transformation to change the underlying monad.
nat :: (forall x. m x -> n x) -> TracerA m a b -> TracerA n a b

-- | Run a tracer only for the Just variant of a Maybe. If it's Nothing,
--   the <a>nullTracer</a> is used (no output).
--   
--   The arrow representation allows for proper laziness: if the tracer
--   parameter does not produce any tracing effects, then the predicate
--   won't even be evaluated. Contrast with the simple contravariant
--   representation as <tt>a -&gt; m ()</tt>, in which the predicate _must_
--   be forced no matter what, because it's impossible to know a priori
--   whether that function will not produce any tracing effects.
--   
--   It's written out explicitly for demonstration. Could also use arrow
--   notation:
--   
--   <pre>
--   traceMaybe p tr = Tracer $ proc a -&gt; do
--     case k a of
--       Just b  -&gt; use tr        -&lt; b
--       Nothing -&gt; Arrow.squelch -&lt; ()
--   </pre>
traceMaybe :: Monad m => (a -> Maybe b) -> Tracer m b -> Tracer m a

-- | Uses <a>traceMaybe</a> to give a tracer which emits only if a
--   predicate is true.
squelchUnless :: Monad m => (a -> Bool) -> Tracer m a -> Tracer m a

-- | The class of contravariant functors.
--   
--   Whereas in Haskell, one can think of a <a>Functor</a> as containing or
--   producing values, a contravariant functor is a functor that can be
--   thought of as <i>consuming</i> values.
--   
--   As an example, consider the type of predicate functions <tt>a -&gt;
--   Bool</tt>. One such predicate might be <tt>negative x = x &lt; 0</tt>,
--   which classifies integers as to whether they are negative. However,
--   given this predicate, we can re-use it in other situations, providing
--   we have a way to map values <i>to</i> integers. For instance, we can
--   use the <tt>negative</tt> predicate on a person's bank balance to work
--   out if they are currently overdrawn:
--   
--   <pre>
--   newtype Predicate a = Predicate { getPredicate :: a -&gt; Bool }
--   
--   instance Contravariant Predicate where
--     contramap :: (a' -&gt; a) -&gt; (Predicate a -&gt; Predicate a')
--     contramap f (Predicate p) = Predicate (p . f)
--                                            |   `- First, map the input...
--                                            `----- then apply the predicate.
--   
--   overdrawn :: Predicate Person
--   overdrawn = contramap personBankBalance negative
--   </pre>
--   
--   Any instance should be subject to the following laws:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><i>Identity</i> <tt><a>contramap</a> <a>id</a> =
--   <a>id</a></tt></li>
--   <li><i>Composition</i> <tt><a>contramap</a> (g . f) = <a>contramap</a>
--   f . <a>contramap</a> g</tt></li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   Note, that the second law follows from the free theorem of the type of
--   <a>contramap</a> and the first law, so you need only check that the
--   former condition holds.
class () => Contravariant (f :: Type -> Type)
contramap :: Contravariant f => (a' -> a) -> f a -> f a'

-- | Replace all locations in the output with the same value. The default
--   definition is <tt><a>contramap</a> . <a>const</a></tt>, but this may
--   be overridden with a more efficient version.
(>$) :: Contravariant f => b -> f b -> f a
infixl 4 >$
instance GHC.Base.Monad m => Data.Functor.Contravariant.Contravariant (Control.Tracer.Tracer m)
instance GHC.Base.Monad m => GHC.Base.Semigroup (Control.Tracer.Tracer m s)
instance GHC.Base.Monad m => GHC.Base.Monoid (Control.Tracer.Tracer m s)

module Cardano.Logging.Types

-- | The Trace carries the underlying tracer Tracer from the contra-tracer
--   package. It adds a <a>LoggingContext</a> and maybe a
--   <a>TraceControl</a> to every message.
newtype Trace m a
Trace :: Tracer m (LoggingContext, Either TraceControl a) -> Trace m a
[unpackTrace] :: Trace m a -> Tracer m (LoggingContext, Either TraceControl a)

-- | Every message needs this to define how to represent itself
class LogFormatting a

-- | Machine readable representation with the possibility to represent with
--   varying serialisations based on the detail level. This will result in
--   JSON formatted log output. A <a>forMachine</a> implementation is
--   required for any instance definition.
forMachine :: LogFormatting a => DetailLevel -> a -> Object

-- | Human-readable representation. The empty text indicates there's no
--   specific human-readable formatting for that type - this is the default
--   implementation. If however human-readble output is explicitly
--   requested, e.g. by logs, the system will fall back to a JSON object
--   conforming to the <a>forMachine</a> definition, and rendering it as a
--   value in `{"data": <a>value</a>}`. Leaving out <a>forHuman</a> in some
--   instance definition will not lead to loss of log information that way.
forHuman :: LogFormatting a => a -> Text

-- | Metrics representation. The default indicates that no metric is based
--   on trace occurrences of that type.
asMetrics :: LogFormatting a => a -> [Metric]
data Metric

-- | An integer metric. Text is used to name the metric
IntM :: Text -> Integer -> Metric

-- | A double metric. Text is used to name the metric
DoubleM :: Text -> Double -> Metric

-- | A counter metric. Text is used to name the metric
CounterM :: Text -> Maybe Int -> Metric

-- | A prometheus metric with key label pairs. Text is used to name the
--   metric [(Text, Text)] is used to represent the key label pairs The
--   value of the metric will always be "1" e.g. if you have a prometheus
--   metric with the name "prometheus_metric" and the key label pairs
--   [("key1", "value1"), ("key2", "value2")] the metric will be
--   represented as "prometheus_metric{key1="value1",key2="value2"} 1"
PrometheusM :: Text -> [(Text, Text)] -> Metric
getMetricName :: Metric -> Text

-- | A helper function for creating an empty |Object|.
emptyObject :: HashMap Text a
newtype Documented a
Documented :: [DocMsg a] -> Documented a
[undoc] :: Documented a -> [DocMsg a]

-- | Document a message by giving a prototype, its most special name in the
--   namespace and a comment in markdown format
data DocMsg a
DocMsg :: Namespace a -> [(Text, Text)] -> Text -> DocMsg a
[dmNamespace] :: DocMsg a -> Namespace a
[dmMetricsMD] :: DocMsg a -> [(Text, Text)]
[dmMarkdown] :: DocMsg a -> Text

-- | Context any log message carries
data LoggingContext
LoggingContext :: [Text] -> [Text] -> Maybe SeverityS -> Maybe Privacy -> Maybe DetailLevel -> LoggingContext
[lcNSInner] :: LoggingContext -> [Text]
[lcNSPrefix] :: LoggingContext -> [Text]
[lcSeverity] :: LoggingContext -> Maybe SeverityS
[lcPrivacy] :: LoggingContext -> Maybe Privacy
[lcDetails] :: LoggingContext -> Maybe DetailLevel
emptyLoggingContext :: LoggingContext

-- | A unique identifier for every message, composed of text A namespace
--   can as well appear with the tracer name (e.g.
--   <a>ChainDB.OpenEvent.OpenedDB</a>), or more prefixes, in this moment
--   it is a NamespaceOuter is used
data Namespace a
Namespace :: [Text] -> [Text] -> Namespace a
[nsPrefix] :: Namespace a -> [Text]
[nsInner] :: Namespace a -> [Text]
nsReplacePrefix :: [Text] -> Namespace a -> Namespace a
nsReplaceInner :: [Text] -> Namespace a -> Namespace a
nsCast :: Namespace a -> Namespace b
nsPrependInner :: Text -> Namespace a -> Namespace b
nsGetComplete :: Namespace a -> [Text]
nsGetTuple :: Namespace a -> ([Text], [Text])
nsRawToText :: ([Text], [Text]) -> Text
nsToText :: Namespace a -> Text
class MetaTrace a
namespaceFor :: MetaTrace a => a -> Namespace a
severityFor :: MetaTrace a => Namespace a -> Maybe a -> Maybe SeverityS
privacyFor :: MetaTrace a => Namespace a -> Maybe a -> Maybe Privacy
detailsFor :: MetaTrace a => Namespace a -> Maybe a -> Maybe DetailLevel
documentFor :: MetaTrace a => Namespace a -> Maybe Text
metricsDocFor :: MetaTrace a => Namespace a -> [(Text, Text)]
allNamespaces :: MetaTrace a => [Namespace a]

-- | Formerly known as verbosity
data DetailLevel
DMinimal :: DetailLevel
DNormal :: DetailLevel
DDetailed :: DetailLevel
DMaximum :: DetailLevel

-- | Privacy of a message. Default is Public
data Privacy

-- | confidential information - handle with care
Confidential :: Privacy

-- | can be public.
Public :: Privacy

-- | Severity of a message
data SeverityS

-- | Debug messages
Debug :: SeverityS

-- | Information
Info :: SeverityS

-- | Normal runtime Conditions
Notice :: SeverityS

-- | General Warnings
Warning :: SeverityS

-- | General Errors
Error :: SeverityS

-- | Severe situations
Critical :: SeverityS

-- | Take immediate action
Alert :: SeverityS

-- | System is unusable
Emergency :: SeverityS

-- | Severity for a filter Nothing means don't show anything (Silence) Just
--   level means show messages with severity &gt;= level
newtype SeverityF
SeverityF :: Maybe SeverityS -> SeverityF
data ConfigOption

-- | Severity level for a filter (default is Warning)
ConfSeverity :: SeverityF -> ConfigOption
[severity] :: ConfigOption -> SeverityF

-- | Detail level (default is DNormal)
ConfDetail :: DetailLevel -> ConfigOption
[detail] :: ConfigOption -> DetailLevel

-- | To which backend to pass Default is [EKGBackend, Forwarder, Stdout
--   MachineFormat]
ConfBackend :: [BackendConfig] -> ConfigOption
[backends] :: ConfigOption -> [BackendConfig]

-- | Construct a limiter with limiting to the Double, which represents
--   frequency in number of messages per second
ConfLimiter :: Double -> ConfigOption
[maxFrequency] :: ConfigOption -> Double
newtype ForwarderAddr
LocalSocket :: FilePath -> ForwarderAddr
data FormatLogging
HumanFormatColoured :: FormatLogging
HumanFormatUncoloured :: FormatLogging
MachineFormat :: FormatLogging
data ForwarderMode

-- | Forwarder works as a client: it initiates network connection with
--   'cardano-tracer' and/or another Haskell acceptor application.
Initiator :: ForwarderMode

-- | Forwarder works as a server: it accepts network connection from
--   'cardano-tracer' and/or another Haskell acceptor application.
Responder :: ForwarderMode
data Verbosity

-- | Maximum verbosity for all tracers in the forwarding protocols.
Maximum :: Verbosity

-- | Minimum verbosity, the forwarding will work as silently as possible.
Minimum :: Verbosity
data TraceOptionForwarder
TraceOptionForwarder :: Word -> Verbosity -> Word -> TraceOptionForwarder
[tofQueueSize] :: TraceOptionForwarder -> Word
[tofVerbosity] :: TraceOptionForwarder -> Verbosity
[tofMaxReconnectDelay] :: TraceOptionForwarder -> Word
defaultForwarder :: TraceOptionForwarder

data ConfigReflection
ConfigReflection :: IORef (Set [Text]) -> IORef (Set [Text]) -> IORef (Set [Text]) -> ConfigReflection
[crSilent] :: ConfigReflection -> IORef (Set [Text])
[crNoMetrics] :: ConfigReflection -> IORef (Set [Text])
[crAllTracers] :: ConfigReflection -> IORef (Set [Text])
emptyConfigReflection :: IO ConfigReflection
data TraceConfig
TraceConfig :: Map [Text] [ConfigOption] -> Maybe TraceOptionForwarder -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> TraceConfig

-- | Options specific to a certain namespace
[tcOptions] :: TraceConfig -> Map [Text] [ConfigOption]

-- | Options for the forwarder
[tcForwarder] :: TraceConfig -> Maybe TraceOptionForwarder

-- | Optional human-readable name of the node.
[tcNodeName] :: TraceConfig -> Maybe Text

-- | Optional prefix for metrics.
[tcMetricsPrefix] :: TraceConfig -> Maybe Text

-- | Optional resource trace frequency in milliseconds.
[tcResourceFrequency] :: TraceConfig -> Maybe Int

-- | Optional ledger metrics frequency in milliseconds.
[tcLedgerMetricsFrequency] :: TraceConfig -> Maybe Int
emptyTraceConfig :: TraceConfig
data FormattedMessage

-- | The bool specifies if the formatting includes colours
FormattedHuman :: Bool -> Text -> FormattedMessage
FormattedMachine :: Text -> FormattedMessage
FormattedMetrics :: [Metric] -> FormattedMessage
FormattedForwarder :: TraceObject -> FormattedMessage
FormattedCBOR :: ByteString -> FormattedMessage

-- | When configuring a net of tracers, it should be run with Config on all
--   entry points first, and then with TCOptimize. When reconfiguring it
--   needs to run TCReset followed by Config followed by TCOptimize
data TraceControl
[TCReset] :: TraceControl
[TCConfig] :: TraceConfig -> TraceControl
[TCOptimize] :: ConfigReflection -> TraceControl
[TCDocument] :: Int -> DocCollector -> TraceControl
newtype DocCollector
DocCollector :: IORef (Map Int LogDoc) -> DocCollector
data LogDoc
LogDoc :: !Text -> !Map Text Text -> ![([Text], [Text])] -> !Maybe SeverityS -> !Maybe Privacy -> !Maybe DetailLevel -> ![DetailLevel] -> ![BackendConfig] -> ![SeverityF] -> ![(Text, Double)] -> Bool -> LogDoc
[ldDoc] :: LogDoc -> !Text
[ldMetricsDoc] :: LogDoc -> !Map Text Text
[ldNamespace] :: LogDoc -> ![([Text], [Text])]
[ldSeverityCoded] :: LogDoc -> !Maybe SeverityS
[ldPrivacyCoded] :: LogDoc -> !Maybe Privacy
[ldDetailsCoded] :: LogDoc -> !Maybe DetailLevel
[ldDetails] :: LogDoc -> ![DetailLevel]
[ldBackends] :: LogDoc -> ![BackendConfig]
[ldFiltered] :: LogDoc -> ![SeverityF]
[ldLimiter] :: LogDoc -> ![(Text, Double)]
[ldSilent] :: LogDoc -> Bool
emptyLogDoc :: Text -> [(Text, Text)] -> LogDoc

data BackendConfig
Forwarder :: BackendConfig
Stdout :: FormatLogging -> BackendConfig
EKGBackend :: BackendConfig
DatapointBackend :: BackendConfig
PrometheusSimple :: Bool -> Maybe HostName -> PortNumber -> BackendConfig

-- | Type for the function foldTraceM from module
--   Cardano<i>Logging</i>Trace
newtype Folding a b
Folding :: b -> Folding a b
unfold :: Folding a b -> b

-- | Used as interface object for ForwarderTracer
data TraceObject
TraceObject :: !Maybe Text -> !Text -> ![Text] -> !SeverityS -> !DetailLevel -> !UTCTime -> !Text -> !Text -> TraceObject
[toHuman] :: TraceObject -> !Maybe Text
[toMachine] :: TraceObject -> !Text
[toNamespace] :: TraceObject -> ![Text]
[toSeverity] :: TraceObject -> !SeverityS
[toDetails] :: TraceObject -> !DetailLevel
[toTimestamp] :: TraceObject -> !UTCTime
[toHostname] :: TraceObject -> !Text
[toThreadId] :: TraceObject -> !Text
data PreFormatted
PreFormatted :: !UTCTime -> !Text -> !Text -> !Maybe Text -> Object -> PreFormatted
[pfTime] :: PreFormatted -> !UTCTime
[pfNamespace] :: PreFormatted -> !Text
[pfThreadId] :: PreFormatted -> !Text
[pfForHuman] :: PreFormatted -> !Maybe Text
[pfForMachineObject] :: PreFormatted -> Object
data HowToConnect

-- | Local pipe (UNIX or Windows).
LocalPipe :: !FilePath -> HowToConnect

-- | Remote socket (host and port).
RemoteSocket :: !Host -> !Port -> HowToConnect
instance GHC.Classes.Eq (Cardano.Logging.Types.Namespace a)

instance GHC.Classes.Eq Cardano.Logging.Types.Metric
instance GHC.Base.Semigroup (Cardano.Logging.Types.Documented a)
instance GHC.Show.Show (Cardano.Logging.Types.Documented a)
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Cardano.Logging.Types.DetailLevel
instance Codec.Serialise.Class.Serialise Cardano.Logging.Types.DetailLevel

instance GHC.Classes.Ord Cardano.Logging.Types.DetailLevel
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Cardano.Logging.Types.DetailLevel
instance Codec.Serialise.Class.Serialise Cardano.Logging.Types.Privacy

instance GHC.Enum.Bounded Cardano.Logging.Types.Privacy
instance GHC.Enum.Enum Cardano.Logging.Types.Privacy

instance GHC.Classes.Ord Cardano.Logging.Types.Privacy
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Cardano.Logging.Types.Privacy

instance GHC.Show.Show Cardano.Logging.Types.TraceObject
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Cardano.Logging.Types.TraceObject
instance GHC.Show.Show Cardano.Logging.Types.FormattedMessage
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Cardano.Logging.Types.FormattedMessage
instance GHC.Show.Show Cardano.Logging.Types.FormatLogging

instance GHC.Classes.Eq Cardano.Logging.Types.ConfigOption
instance GHC.Show.Show Cardano.Logging.Types.ForwarderAddr
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Cardano.Logging.Types.ForwarderAddr
