instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Cardano.Logging.Types.DetailLevel
instance GHC.Show.Show (Cardano.Logging.Types.DocMsg a)
instance GHC.Show.Show (Cardano.Logging.Types.Namespace a)

module Cardano.Logging.Types.TraceMessage

-- | base for a machine readable trace message (JSON or CBOR), with
--   metadata, and enclosed payload data from the trace itself.
data TraceMessage
TraceMessage :: !UTCTime -> !Text -> !Object -> !SeverityS -> !Text -> !Text -> TraceMessage
[tmsgAt] :: TraceMessage -> !UTCTime
[tmsgNS] :: TraceMessage -> !Text
[tmsgData] :: TraceMessage -> !Object
[tmsgSev] :: TraceMessage -> !SeverityS
[tmsgThread] :: TraceMessage -> !Text
[tmsgHost] :: TraceMessage -> !Text
instance GHC.Show.Show Cardano.Logging.Types.TraceMessage.TraceMessage
instance Codec.Serialise.Class.Serialise Cardano.Logging.Types.TraceMessage.TraceMessage
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Cardano.Logging.Types.TraceMessage.TraceMessage
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Cardano.Logging.Types.TraceMessage.TraceMessage
instance Codec.Serialise.Class.Serialise Data.Aeson.Types.Internal.Object


-- | Run a simple Prometheus TCP server, responding *only* to the
--   '/metrics' URL with current Node metrics
module Cardano.Logging.Prometheus.TCPServer
runPrometheusSimple :: Tracer IO TracePrometheusSimple -> Store -> (Bool, Maybe HostName, PortNumber) -> IO (Async ())
runPrometheusSimpleSilent :: Store -> (Bool, Maybe HostName, PortNumber) -> IO (Async ())
data TracePrometheusSimple
TracePrometheusSimpleStart :: Word16 -> TracePrometheusSimple
[port] :: TracePrometheusSimple -> Word16
TracePrometheusSimpleStop :: String -> TracePrometheusSimple
[message] :: TracePrometheusSimple -> String
instance GHC.Show.Show Cardano.Logging.Prometheus.TCPServer.TracePrometheusSimple
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Cardano.Logging.Prometheus.TCPServer.Method
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Cardano.Logging.Prometheus.TCPServer.Accept
instance Cardano.Logging.Types.LogFormatting Cardano.Logging.Prometheus.TCPServer.TracePrometheusSimple

module Cardano.Logging.ConfigurationParser

-- | Read a configuration file and returns the internal representation
readConfiguration :: FilePath -> IO TraceConfig

-- | Read a configuration file and returns the internal representation Uses
--   values which are not in the file from the defaultConfig
readConfigurationWithDefault :: FilePath -> TraceConfig -> IO TraceConfig

-- | Convert from internal to external representation
configToRepresentation :: TraceConfig -> ConfigRepresentation
instance GHC.Show.Show Cardano.Logging.ConfigurationParser.ConfigOptionRep
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Cardano.Logging.ConfigurationParser.ConfigOptionRep
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Cardano.Logging.ConfigurationParser.ConfigOptionRep
instance GHC.Show.Show Cardano.Logging.ConfigurationParser.ConfigRepresentation
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Cardano.Logging.ConfigurationParser.ConfigRepresentation
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Cardano.Logging.ConfigurationParser.ConfigRepresentation
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Cardano.Logging.ConfigurationParser.ConfigRepresentation
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Cardano.Logging.ConfigurationParser.ConfigRepresentation
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Cardano.Logging.ConfigurationParser.ConfigOptionRep
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Cardano.Logging.ConfigurationParser.ConfigOptionRep
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Cardano.Logging.Types.TraceConfig

module Cardano.Logging.TraceDispatcherMessage
data UnknownNamespaceKind
UKFSeverity :: UnknownNamespaceKind
UKFPrivacy :: UnknownNamespaceKind
UKFDetails :: UnknownNamespaceKind
data TraceDispatcherMessage

-- | This message indicates the start of frequency limiting
StartLimiting :: Text -> TraceDispatcherMessage

-- | This message indicates the stop of frequency limiting, and gives the
--   number of messages that has been suppressed
StopLimiting :: Text -> Int -> TraceDispatcherMessage

-- | This message remembers of ongoing frequency limiting, and gives the
--   number of messages that has been suppressed
RememberLimiting :: Text -> Int -> TraceDispatcherMessage

-- | An internal error was detected
UnknownNamespace :: [Text] -> [Text] -> UnknownNamespaceKind -> TraceDispatcherMessage

-- | The first array signifies the namespace of silent tracers The second
--   array signifies the namespace tracers without metrics The third array
--   gives the names of all tracers
TracerInfo :: [Text] -> [Text] -> [Text] -> TraceDispatcherMessage

-- | Outputs optional statistics about metrics frequency
MetricsInfo :: Map Text Int -> TraceDispatcherMessage

-- | Consistency check found warnings
TracerConsistencyWarnings :: [Text] -> TraceDispatcherMessage

-- | Trace the effective configuration as JSON
TracerInfoConfig :: TraceConfig -> TraceDispatcherMessage
instance GHC.Show.Show Cardano.Logging.TraceDispatcherMessage.TraceDispatcherMessage
instance Cardano.Logging.Types.LogFormatting Cardano.Logging.TraceDispatcherMessage.TraceDispatcherMessage
instance Cardano.Logging.Types.MetaTrace Cardano.Logging.TraceDispatcherMessage.TraceDispatcherMessage
instance GHC.Show.Show Cardano.Logging.TraceDispatcherMessage.UnknownNamespaceKind

module Cardano.Logging.Consistency

-- | Warnings as a list of text
type NSWarnings = [Text]

-- | Checks if all namespaces in this configuration are legal. Legal in
--   this case means that it can be found by a hierarchical lookup in all
--   namespaces. Warns if namespaces in all namespaces are not unique,
--   Warns if namespaces in all namespaces are ending in the middle of
--   another namespace. The namespaces in allNamespaces are consistent with
--   the namespaces for the severityFor, privacyFor, detailsFor,
--   documentFor and metricsDocFor functions.
checkTraceConfiguration :: FilePath -> TraceConfig -> [([Text], [Text])] -> IO NSWarnings
checkTraceConfiguration' :: TraceConfig -> [([Text], [Text])] -> NSWarnings
instance GHC.Show.Show Cardano.Logging.Consistency.NSLookup

module Cardano.Logging.Trace

-- | Adds a message object to a trace
traceWith :: Monad m => Trace m a -> a -> m ()

-- | Sets a new logging context for this message
withLoggingContext :: Monad m => LoggingContext -> Trace m a -> Trace m a
filterTrace :: Monad m => ((LoggingContext, a) -> Bool) -> Trace m a -> Trace m a
filterTraceMaybe :: Monad m => Trace m a -> Trace m (Maybe a)
filterTraceBySeverity :: Monad m => Maybe SeverityF -> Trace m a -> Trace m a
filterTraceByPrivacy :: Monad m => Maybe Privacy -> Trace m a -> Trace m a

-- | Sets severity for the messages in this trace
setSeverity :: Monad m => SeverityS -> Trace m a -> Trace m a

-- | Sets severities for the messages in this trace based on the MetaTrace
--   class
withSeverity :: forall m a. (Monad m, MetaTrace a) => Trace m a -> Trace m a

-- | Sets privacy Confidential for the messages in this trace
privately :: Monad m => Trace m a -> Trace m a

-- | Sets privacy for the messages in this trace
setPrivacy :: Monad m => Privacy -> Trace m a -> Trace m a

-- | Sets privacy for the messages in this trace based on the MetaTrace
--   class
withPrivacy :: forall m a. (Monad m, MetaTrace a) => Trace m a -> Trace m a
allPublic :: a -> Privacy
allConfidential :: a -> Privacy

-- | Sets detail level for the messages in this trace
setDetails :: Monad m => DetailLevel -> Trace m a -> Trace m a

-- | Sets detail level for the messages in this trace based on the message
withDetails :: forall m a. (Monad m, MetaTrace a) => Trace m a -> Trace m a

-- | Contramap a monadic function over a trace
contramapM :: Monad m => Trace m b -> ((LoggingContext, Either TraceControl a) -> m (LoggingContext, Either TraceControl b)) -> m (Trace m a)

-- | Contramap a monadic function over a trace Can as well filter out
--   messages
contramapMCond :: Monad m => Trace m b -> ((LoggingContext, Either TraceControl a) -> m (Maybe (LoggingContext, Either TraceControl b))) -> m (Trace m a)
contramapM' :: Monad m => ((LoggingContext, Either TraceControl a) -> m ()) -> Trace m a

-- | Folds the monadic cata function with acc over a. Uses an MVar to store
--   the state
foldTraceM :: forall a acc m. MonadUnliftIO m => (acc -> LoggingContext -> a -> m acc) -> acc -> Trace m (Folding a acc) -> m (Trace m a)

-- | Like foldTraceM, but filter the trace by a predicate.
foldCondTraceM :: forall a acc m. MonadUnliftIO m => (acc -> LoggingContext -> a -> m acc) -> acc -> (a -> Bool) -> Trace m (Folding a acc) -> m (Trace m a)

-- | Allows to route to different tracers, based on the message being
--   processed. The second argument must mappend all possible tracers of
--   the first argument to one tracer. This is required for the
--   configuration!
routingTrace :: forall m a. Monad m => (a -> m (Trace m a)) -> Trace m a -> Trace m a

-- | Sets names for the messages in this trace based on the selector
--   function and appends the provided names to the context.
withNames :: forall m a. (Monad m, MetaTrace a) => [Text] -> Trace m a -> Trace m a

-- | Appends a name to the context. E.g. appendName "specific" $ appendName
--   "middle" $ appendName "general" tracer give the result:
--   `general.middle.specific`.
appendPrefixName :: Monad m => Text -> Trace m a -> Trace m a

-- | Appends all names to the context.
appendPrefixNames :: Monad m => [Text] -> Trace m a -> Trace m a
appendInnerName :: Monad m => Text -> Trace m a -> Trace m a

-- | Appends all names to the context.
appendInnerNames :: Monad m => [Text] -> Trace m a -> Trace m a

-- | Sets names for the messages in this trace based on the selector
--   function
withInnerNames :: forall m a. (Monad m, MetaTrace a) => Trace m a -> Trace m a

-- | A contramap' which is strict in its second argument and its result
--   captures a common pattern to avoid unintentionally leaking space when
--   composing tracers. The infix alias is (&gt;!$!&lt;).
contramap' :: Contravariant f => (a' -> a) -> f a -> f a'

-- | A contramap' which is strict in its second argument and its result
--   captures a common pattern to avoid unintentionally leaking space when
--   composing tracers. The infix alias is (&gt;!$!&lt;).
(>!$!<) :: Contravariant f => (a' -> a) -> f a -> f a'
infixl 4 >!$!<

module Cardano.Logging.FrequencyLimiter

-- | Limits the frequency of messages to nMsg which is given per minute.
--   
--   If the limiter detects more messages, it traces randomly selected
--   messages with the given frequency on the <tt>vtracer</tt> until the
--   frequency falls under the threshold long enough.(see below)
--   
--   Before this the <tt>ltracer</tt> gets a <a>StartLimiting</a> message.
--   In-between you receive <tt>ContinueLimiting</tt> messages on the
--   <tt>ltracer</tt> every <a>reminderPeriod</a> seconds, with the number
--   of suppressed messages. Finally it sends a <a>StopLimiting</a> message
--   on the <tt>ltracer</tt> and traces all messages on the
--   <tt>vtracer</tt> again.
--   
--   A budget is used to decide when to start limiting and stop limiting,
--   so that the limiter does not get activated if few messages are send in
--   high frequency, and doesn't get deactivated if their are only few
--   messages which come with low frequency. When messages arrive in
--   shorter frequency then by the given <tt>thresholdFrequency</tt> budget
--   is earned, and if they arrive in a longer period budget is spend. If
--   budget is gets higher then <a>budgetLimit</a>, the limiter starts, and
--   if it falls below minus <a>budgetLimit</a> the limiter stops.
limitFrequency :: forall a m. MonadUnliftIO m => Double -> Text -> Trace m TraceDispatcherMessage -> Trace m a -> m (Trace m a)
data LimiterSpec
LimiterSpec :: [Text] -> Text -> Double -> LimiterSpec
[lsNs] :: LimiterSpec -> [Text]
[lsName] :: LimiterSpec -> Text
[lsFrequency] :: LimiterSpec -> Double
instance (GHC.Show.Show a, GHC.Show.Show b) => GHC.Show.Show (Cardano.Logging.FrequencyLimiter.MaybeTuple' a b)
instance GHC.Show.Show a => GHC.Show.Show (Cardano.Logging.FrequencyLimiter.FrequencyRec a)

module Cardano.Logging.Formatter

-- | Format this trace as metrics
metricsFormatter :: forall a m. (LogFormatting a, MonadIO m) => Trace m FormattedMessage -> Trace m a

-- | Transform this trace to a preformatted message, so that double
--   serialization is avoided
preFormatted :: (LogFormatting a, MonadIO m) => Bool -> Trace m PreFormatted -> m (Trace m a)
forwardFormatter :: forall a m. MonadIO m => LogFormatting a => Trace m FormattedMessage -> m (Trace m a)

-- | Format this trace as TraceObject for the trace forwarder
forwardFormatter' :: forall m. MonadIO m => Trace m FormattedMessage -> Trace m PreFormatted
machineFormatter :: forall a m. (MonadIO m, LogFormatting a) => Trace m FormattedMessage -> m (Trace m a)

-- | Format this trace as TraceObject for machine-readable text output
--   (JSON)
machineFormatter' :: forall m. MonadIO m => Trace m FormattedMessage -> Trace m PreFormatted
cborFormatter :: forall a m. (MonadIO m, LogFormatting a) => Trace m FormattedMessage -> m (Trace m a)

-- | Format this trace in binary serialisation (CBOR)
cborFormatter' :: forall m. MonadIO m => Trace m FormattedMessage -> Trace m PreFormatted
humanFormatter :: forall a m. MonadIO m => LogFormatting a => Bool -> Trace m FormattedMessage -> m (Trace m a)

-- | Format this trace in human readable text output
humanFormatter' :: forall m. MonadIO m => Bool -> Trace m FormattedMessage -> Trace m PreFormatted

module Cardano.Logging.DocuGenerator
documentTracer :: forall a. MetaTrace a => Trace IO a -> IO DocTracer
documentTracer' :: forall a a1. MetaTrace a => (Trace IO a1 -> IO (Trace IO a)) -> Trace IO a1 -> IO DocTracer
docuResultsToText :: DocTracer -> TraceConfig -> Text
docuResultsToMetricsHelptext :: DocTracer -> Text
docTracer :: MonadIO m => BackendConfig -> Trace m FormattedMessage
docTracerDatapoint :: MonadIO m => BackendConfig -> Trace m a

-- | Callback for doc collection
docIt :: MonadIO m => BackendConfig -> (LoggingContext, Either TraceControl a) -> m ()

-- | Callback for doc collection
addFiltered :: MonadIO m => TraceControl -> Maybe SeverityF -> m ()

-- | Callback for doc collection
addLimiter :: MonadIO m => TraceControl -> (Text, Double) -> m ()
addSilent :: MonadIO m => TraceControl -> Maybe Bool -> m ()

-- | Convenience function for adding a namespace prefix to a documented
addDocumentedNamespace :: [Text] -> Documented a -> Documented a
data DocuResult
data DocTracer
DocTracer :: [[Text]] -> [[Text]] -> [[Text]] -> [([Text], DocuResult)] -> [InconsistencyWarning] -> DocTracer
[dtTracerNames] :: DocTracer -> [[Text]]
[dtSilent] :: DocTracer -> [[Text]]
[dtNoMetrics] :: DocTracer -> [[Text]]
[dtBuilderList] :: DocTracer -> [([Text], DocuResult)]
[dtWarnings] :: DocTracer -> [InconsistencyWarning]
instance GHC.Show.Show Cardano.Logging.DocuGenerator.DocTracer
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Cardano.Logging.DocuGenerator.MetricsHelp
instance GHC.Base.Semigroup Cardano.Logging.DocuGenerator.DocTracer

module Cardano.Logging.Tracer.Standard

-- | The standardTracer handles stdout logging in a thread-safe manner. It
--   is strongly advised to construct only one standardTracer for any
--   application.
standardTracer :: forall m. MonadIO m => m (Trace m FormattedMessage)

module Cardano.Logging.Tracer.Forward

-- | It is mandatory to construct only one forwardTracer tracer in any
--   application! Throwing away a forwardTracer tracer and using a new one
--   will result in an exception
forwardTracer :: forall m. MonadIO m => (TraceObject -> IO ()) -> Trace m FormattedMessage

module Cardano.Logging.Tracer.EKG

-- | It is mandatory to construct only one standard tracer in any
--   application! Throwing away a standard tracer and using a new one will
--   result in an exception
ekgTracer :: MonadIO m => TraceConfig -> Store -> m (Trace m FormattedMessage)

module Cardano.Logging.Tracer.DataPoint

-- | Type wrapper for some value of type <tt>v</tt>. The only reason we
--   need this wrapper is an ability to store different values in the same
--   <a>DataPointStore</a>.
--   
--   Please note that when the acceptor application will read the value of
--   type <tt>v</tt> from the store, this value is just as unstructured
--   JSON, but not Haskell value of type <tt>v</tt>. That's why
--   <a>FromJSON</a> instance for type <tt>v</tt> should be available for
--   the acceptor application, to decode unstructured JSON.
data DataPoint
[DataPoint] :: (ToJSON v, NFData v) => !v -> DataPoint
type DataPointName = Text
type DataPointStore = TVar (Map DataPointName DataPoint)
initDataPointStore :: IO DataPointStore

-- | Write <a>DataPoint</a> to the store.
writeToStore :: DataPointStore -> DataPointName -> DataPoint -> IO ()
dataPointTracer :: forall m. MonadIO m => DataPointStore -> Trace m DataPoint
mkDataPointTracer :: forall dp. (ToJSON dp, MetaTrace dp, NFData dp) => Trace IO DataPoint -> IO (Trace IO dp)

module Cardano.Logging.Configuration

data ConfigReflection
ConfigReflection :: IORef (Set [Text]) -> IORef (Set [Text]) -> IORef (Set [Text]) -> ConfigReflection
[crSilent] :: ConfigReflection -> IORef (Set [Text])
[crNoMetrics] :: ConfigReflection -> IORef (Set [Text])
[crAllTracers] :: ConfigReflection -> IORef (Set [Text])
emptyConfigReflection :: IO ConfigReflection

-- | Call this function at initialisation, and later for reconfiguration.
--   Config reflection is used to optimise the tracers and has to collect
--   information about the tracers. Although it is possible to give more
--   then one tracer of the same time, it is not a common case to do this.
configureTracers :: forall a m. (MetaTrace a, MonadIO m) => ConfigReflection -> TraceConfig -> [Trace m a] -> m ()

-- | Take a selector function called <tt>extract</tt>. Take a function from
--   trace to trace with this config dependent value. In this way construct
--   a trace transformer with a config value
withNamespaceConfig :: forall m a b c. (MonadIO m, Ord b) => String -> (TraceConfig -> Namespace a -> m b) -> (Maybe b -> Trace m c -> m (Trace m a)) -> Trace m c -> m (Trace m a)

-- | Filter a trace by severity and take the filter value from the config
filterSeverityFromConfig :: MonadIO m => Trace m a -> m (Trace m a)

-- | Set detail level of a trace from the config
withDetailsFromConfig :: MonadIO m => Trace m a -> m (Trace m a)

-- | Routing and formatting of a trace from the config
withBackendsFromConfig :: MonadIO m => (Maybe [BackendConfig] -> Trace m FormattedMessage -> m (Trace m a)) -> m (Trace m a)

-- | Routing and formatting of a trace from the config
withLimitersFromConfig :: forall a m. MonadUnliftIO m => Trace m TraceDispatcherMessage -> Trace m a -> m (Trace m a)

-- | Switch off any message of a particular tracer based on the
--   configuration. If the top tracer is silent and no subtracer is not
--   silent, then switch it off
maybeSilent :: forall m a. MonadIO m => (TraceConfig -> Namespace a -> Bool) -> [Text] -> Bool -> Trace m a -> m (Trace m a)
isSilentTracer :: forall a. MetaTrace a => TraceConfig -> Namespace a -> Bool
hasNoMetrics :: forall a. MetaTrace a => TraceConfig -> Namespace a -> Bool

-- | If no severity can be found in the config, it is set to Warning
getSeverity :: TraceConfig -> Namespace a -> SeverityF

-- | If no details can be found in the config, it is set to DNormal
getDetails :: TraceConfig -> Namespace a -> DetailLevel

-- | If no backends can be found in the config, it is set to [EKGBackend,
--   Forwarder, Stdout HumanFormatColoured]
getBackends :: TraceConfig -> Namespace a -> [BackendConfig]
instance GHC.Classes.Eq (Cardano.Logging.Configuration.Limiter m a)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord (Cardano.Logging.Configuration.Limiter m a)
instance GHC.Show.Show (Cardano.Logging.Configuration.Limiter m a)

module Cardano.Logging.Tracer.Composed

-- | Construct a tracer according to the requirements for cardano node. The
--   tracer gets a <tt>name</tt>, which is appended to its namespace. The
--   tracer has to be an instance of LogFormatting for the display of
--   messages and an instance of MetaTrace for meta information such as
--   severity, privacy, details and backends'. The tracer gets the
--   backends': <tt>trStdout</tt>, <tt>trForward</tt> and <tt>mbTrEkg</tt>
--   as arguments. The returned tracer needs to be configured with a
--   configuration before it is used.
mkCardanoTracer :: forall evt. (LogFormatting evt, MetaTrace evt) => Trace IO FormattedMessage -> Trace IO FormattedMessage -> Maybe (Trace IO FormattedMessage) -> [Text] -> IO (Trace IO evt)

-- | Adds the possibility to add special tracers via the hook function
mkCardanoTracer' :: forall evt evt1. (LogFormatting evt1, MetaTrace evt1) => Trace IO FormattedMessage -> Trace IO FormattedMessage -> Maybe (Trace IO FormattedMessage) -> [Text] -> (Trace IO evt1 -> IO (Trace IO evt)) -> IO (Trace IO evt)
mkMetricsTracer :: Maybe (Trace IO FormattedMessage) -> Trace IO FormattedMessage
traceTracerInfo :: Trace IO FormattedMessage -> Trace IO FormattedMessage -> ConfigReflection -> IO ()
traceConfigWarnings :: Trace IO FormattedMessage -> Trace IO FormattedMessage -> [Text] -> IO ()
traceEffectiveConfiguration :: Trace IO FormattedMessage -> Trace IO FormattedMessage -> TraceConfig -> IO ()

module Cardano.Logging

-- | The class of contravariant functors.
--   
--   Whereas in Haskell, one can think of a <a>Functor</a> as containing or
--   producing values, a contravariant functor is a functor that can be
--   thought of as <i>consuming</i> values.
--   
--   As an example, consider the type of predicate functions <tt>a -&gt;
--   Bool</tt>. One such predicate might be <tt>negative x = x &lt; 0</tt>,
--   which classifies integers as to whether they are negative. However,
--   given this predicate, we can re-use it in other situations, providing
--   we have a way to map values <i>to</i> integers. For instance, we can
--   use the <tt>negative</tt> predicate on a person's bank balance to work
--   out if they are currently overdrawn:
--   
--   <pre>
--   newtype Predicate a = Predicate { getPredicate :: a -&gt; Bool }
--   
--   instance Contravariant Predicate where
--     contramap :: (a' -&gt; a) -&gt; (Predicate a -&gt; Predicate a')
--     contramap f (Predicate p) = Predicate (p . f)
--                                            |   `- First, map the input...
--                                            `----- then apply the predicate.
--   
--   overdrawn :: Predicate Person
--   overdrawn = contramap personBankBalance negative
--   </pre>
--   
--   Any instance should be subject to the following laws:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><i>Identity</i> <tt><a>contramap</a> <a>id</a> =
--   <a>id</a></tt></li>
--   <li><i>Composition</i> <tt><a>contramap</a> (g . f) = <a>contramap</a>
--   f . <a>contramap</a> g</tt></li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   Note, that the second law follows from the free theorem of the type of
--   <a>contramap</a> and the first law, so you need only check that the
--   former condition holds.
class () => Contravariant (f :: Type -> Type)
contramap :: Contravariant f => (a' -> a) -> f a -> f a'

-- | Replace all locations in the output with the same value. The default
--   definition is <tt><a>contramap</a> . <a>const</a></tt>, but this may
--   be overridden with a more efficient version.
(>$) :: Contravariant f => b -> f b -> f a
infixl 4 >$

-- | The Trace carries the underlying tracer Tracer from the contra-tracer
--   package. It adds a <a>LoggingContext</a> and maybe a
--   <a>TraceControl</a> to every message.
newtype Trace m a
Trace :: Tracer m (LoggingContext, Either TraceControl a) -> Trace m a
[unpackTrace] :: Trace m a -> Tracer m (LoggingContext, Either TraceControl a)
data TraceDispatcherMessage

-- | This message indicates the start of frequency limiting
StartLimiting :: Text -> TraceDispatcherMessage

-- | This message indicates the stop of frequency limiting, and gives the
--   number of messages that has been suppressed
StopLimiting :: Text -> Int -> TraceDispatcherMessage

-- | This message remembers of ongoing frequency limiting, and gives the
--   number of messages that has been suppressed
RememberLimiting :: Text -> Int -> TraceDispatcherMessage

-- | An internal error was detected
UnknownNamespace :: [Text] -> [Text] -> UnknownNamespaceKind -> TraceDispatcherMessage

-- | The first array signifies the namespace of silent tracers The second
--   array signifies the namespace tracers without metrics The third array
--   gives the names of all tracers
TracerInfo :: [Text] -> [Text] -> [Text] -> TraceDispatcherMessage

-- | Outputs optional statistics about metrics frequency
MetricsInfo :: Map Text Int -> TraceDispatcherMessage

-- | Consistency check found warnings
TracerConsistencyWarnings :: [Text] -> TraceDispatcherMessage

-- | Trace the effective configuration as JSON
TracerInfoConfig :: TraceConfig -> TraceDispatcherMessage

-- | Type wrapper for some value of type <tt>v</tt>. The only reason we
--   need this wrapper is an ability to store different values in the same
--   <a>DataPointStore</a>.
--   
--   Please note that when the acceptor application will read the value of
--   type <tt>v</tt> from the store, this value is just as unstructured
--   JSON, but not Haskell value of type <tt>v</tt>. That's why
--   <a>FromJSON</a> instance for type <tt>v</tt> should be available for
--   the acceptor application, to decode unstructured JSON.
data DataPoint
[DataPoint] :: (ToJSON v, NFData v) => !v -> DataPoint

data ConfigReflection
ConfigReflection :: IORef (Set [Text]) -> IORef (Set [Text]) -> IORef (Set [Text]) -> ConfigReflection
[crSilent] :: ConfigReflection -> IORef (Set [Text])
[crNoMetrics] :: ConfigReflection -> IORef (Set [Text])
[crAllTracers] :: ConfigReflection -> IORef (Set [Text])

-- | Warnings as a list of text
type NSWarnings = [Text]
data DocuResult
data DocTracer
DocTracer :: [[Text]] -> [[Text]] -> [[Text]] -> [([Text], DocuResult)] -> [InconsistencyWarning] -> DocTracer
[dtTracerNames] :: DocTracer -> [[Text]]
[dtSilent] :: DocTracer -> [[Text]]
[dtNoMetrics] :: DocTracer -> [[Text]]
[dtBuilderList] :: DocTracer -> [([Text], DocuResult)]
[dtWarnings] :: DocTracer -> [InconsistencyWarning]
data LimiterSpec
LimiterSpec :: [Text] -> Text -> Double -> LimiterSpec
[lsNs] :: LimiterSpec -> [Text]
[lsName] :: LimiterSpec -> Text
[lsFrequency] :: LimiterSpec -> Double
data UnknownNamespaceKind
UKFSeverity :: UnknownNamespaceKind
UKFPrivacy :: UnknownNamespaceKind
UKFDetails :: UnknownNamespaceKind
type DataPointName = Text
type DataPointStore = TVar (Map DataPointName DataPoint)

-- | Every message needs this to define how to represent itself
class LogFormatting a

-- | Machine readable representation with the possibility to represent with
--   varying serialisations based on the detail level. This will result in
--   JSON formatted log output. A <a>forMachine</a> implementation is
--   required for any instance definition.
forMachine :: LogFormatting a => DetailLevel -> a -> Object

-- | Human-readable representation. The empty text indicates there's no
--   specific human-readable formatting for that type - this is the default
--   implementation. If however human-readble output is explicitly
--   requested, e.g. by logs, the system will fall back to a JSON object
--   conforming to the <a>forMachine</a> definition, and rendering it as a
--   value in `{"data": <a>value</a>}`. Leaving out <a>forHuman</a> in some
--   instance definition will not lead to loss of log information that way.
forHuman :: LogFormatting a => a -> Text

-- | Metrics representation. The default indicates that no metric is based
--   on trace occurrences of that type.
asMetrics :: LogFormatting a => a -> [Metric]
data Metric

-- | An integer metric. Text is used to name the metric
IntM :: Text -> Integer -> Metric

-- | A double metric. Text is used to name the metric
DoubleM :: Text -> Double -> Metric

-- | A counter metric. Text is used to name the metric
CounterM :: Text -> Maybe Int -> Metric

-- | A prometheus metric with key label pairs. Text is used to name the
--   metric [(Text, Text)] is used to represent the key label pairs The
--   value of the metric will always be "1" e.g. if you have a prometheus
--   metric with the name "prometheus_metric" and the key label pairs
--   [("key1", "value1"), ("key2", "value2")] the metric will be
--   represented as "prometheus_metric{key1="value1",key2="value2"} 1"
PrometheusM :: Text -> [(Text, Text)] -> Metric
newtype Documented a
Documented :: [DocMsg a] -> Documented a
[undoc] :: Documented a -> [DocMsg a]

-- | Document a message by giving a prototype, its most special name in the
--   namespace and a comment in markdown format
data DocMsg a
DocMsg :: Namespace a -> [(Text, Text)] -> Text -> DocMsg a
[dmNamespace] :: DocMsg a -> Namespace a
[dmMetricsMD] :: DocMsg a -> [(Text, Text)]
[dmMarkdown] :: DocMsg a -> Text

-- | Context any log message carries
data LoggingContext
LoggingContext :: [Text] -> [Text] -> Maybe SeverityS -> Maybe Privacy -> Maybe DetailLevel -> LoggingContext
[lcNSInner] :: LoggingContext -> [Text]
[lcNSPrefix] :: LoggingContext -> [Text]
[lcSeverity] :: LoggingContext -> Maybe SeverityS
[lcPrivacy] :: LoggingContext -> Maybe Privacy
[lcDetails] :: LoggingContext -> Maybe DetailLevel

-- | A unique identifier for every message, composed of text A namespace
--   can as well appear with the tracer name (e.g.
--   <a>ChainDB.OpenEvent.OpenedDB</a>), or more prefixes, in this moment
--   it is a NamespaceOuter is used
data Namespace a
Namespace :: [Text] -> [Text] -> Namespace a
[nsPrefix] :: Namespace a -> [Text]
[nsInner] :: Namespace a -> [Text]
class MetaTrace a
namespaceFor :: MetaTrace a => a -> Namespace a
severityFor :: MetaTrace a => Namespace a -> Maybe a -> Maybe SeverityS
privacyFor :: MetaTrace a => Namespace a -> Maybe a -> Maybe Privacy
detailsFor :: MetaTrace a => Namespace a -> Maybe a -> Maybe DetailLevel
documentFor :: MetaTrace a => Namespace a -> Maybe Text
metricsDocFor :: MetaTrace a => Namespace a -> [(Text, Text)]
allNamespaces :: MetaTrace a => [Namespace a]

-- | Formerly known as verbosity
data DetailLevel
DMinimal :: DetailLevel
DNormal :: DetailLevel
DDetailed :: DetailLevel
DMaximum :: DetailLevel

-- | Privacy of a message. Default is Public
data Privacy

-- | confidential information - handle with care
Confidential :: Privacy

-- | can be public.
Public :: Privacy

-- | Severity of a message
data SeverityS

-- | Debug messages
Debug :: SeverityS

-- | Information
Info :: SeverityS

-- | Normal runtime Conditions
Notice :: SeverityS

-- | General Warnings
Warning :: SeverityS

-- | General Errors
Error :: SeverityS

-- | Severe situations
Critical :: SeverityS

-- | Take immediate action
Alert :: SeverityS

-- | System is unusable
Emergency :: SeverityS

-- | Severity for a filter Nothing means don't show anything (Silence) Just
--   level means show messages with severity &gt;= level
newtype SeverityF
SeverityF :: Maybe SeverityS -> SeverityF
data ConfigOption

-- | Severity level for a filter (default is Warning)
ConfSeverity :: SeverityF -> ConfigOption
[severity] :: ConfigOption -> SeverityF

-- | Detail level (default is DNormal)
ConfDetail :: DetailLevel -> ConfigOption
[detail] :: ConfigOption -> DetailLevel

-- | To which backend to pass Default is [EKGBackend, Forwarder, Stdout
--   MachineFormat]
ConfBackend :: [BackendConfig] -> ConfigOption
[backends] :: ConfigOption -> [BackendConfig]

-- | Construct a limiter with limiting to the Double, which represents
--   frequency in number of messages per second
ConfLimiter :: Double -> ConfigOption
[maxFrequency] :: ConfigOption -> Double
newtype ForwarderAddr
LocalSocket :: FilePath -> ForwarderAddr
data FormatLogging
HumanFormatColoured :: FormatLogging
HumanFormatUncoloured :: FormatLogging
MachineFormat :: FormatLogging
data ForwarderMode

-- | Forwarder works as a client: it initiates network connection with
--   'cardano-tracer' and/or another Haskell acceptor application.
Initiator :: ForwarderMode

-- | Forwarder works as a server: it accepts network connection from
--   'cardano-tracer' and/or another Haskell acceptor application.
Responder :: ForwarderMode
data Verbosity

-- | Maximum verbosity for all tracers in the forwarding protocols.
Maximum :: Verbosity

-- | Minimum verbosity, the forwarding will work as silently as possible.
Minimum :: Verbosity
data TraceOptionForwarder
TraceOptionForwarder :: Word -> Verbosity -> Word -> TraceOptionForwarder
[tofQueueSize] :: TraceOptionForwarder -> Word
[tofVerbosity] :: TraceOptionForwarder -> Verbosity
[tofMaxReconnectDelay] :: TraceOptionForwarder -> Word
data TraceConfig
TraceConfig :: Map [Text] [ConfigOption] -> Maybe TraceOptionForwarder -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> TraceConfig

-- | Options specific to a certain namespace
[tcOptions] :: TraceConfig -> Map [Text] [ConfigOption]

-- | Options for the forwarder
[tcForwarder] :: TraceConfig -> Maybe TraceOptionForwarder

-- | Optional human-readable name of the node.
[tcNodeName] :: TraceConfig -> Maybe Text

-- | Optional prefix for metrics.
[tcMetricsPrefix] :: TraceConfig -> Maybe Text

-- | Optional resource trace frequency in milliseconds.
[tcResourceFrequency] :: TraceConfig -> Maybe Int

-- | Optional ledger metrics frequency in milliseconds.
[tcLedgerMetricsFrequency] :: TraceConfig -> Maybe Int
data FormattedMessage

-- | The bool specifies if the formatting includes colours
FormattedHuman :: Bool -> Text -> FormattedMessage
FormattedMachine :: Text -> FormattedMessage
FormattedMetrics :: [Metric] -> FormattedMessage
FormattedForwarder :: TraceObject -> FormattedMessage
FormattedCBOR :: ByteString -> FormattedMessage

-- | When configuring a net of tracers, it should be run with Config on all
--   entry points first, and then with TCOptimize. When reconfiguring it
--   needs to run TCReset followed by Config followed by TCOptimize
data TraceControl
[TCReset] :: TraceControl
[TCConfig] :: TraceConfig -> TraceControl
[TCOptimize] :: ConfigReflection -> TraceControl
[TCDocument] :: Int -> DocCollector -> TraceControl
newtype DocCollector
DocCollector :: IORef (Map Int LogDoc) -> DocCollector
data LogDoc
LogDoc :: !Text -> !Map Text Text -> ![([Text], [Text])] -> !Maybe SeverityS -> !Maybe Privacy -> !Maybe DetailLevel -> ![DetailLevel] -> ![BackendConfig] -> ![SeverityF] -> ![(Text, Double)] -> Bool -> LogDoc
[ldDoc] :: LogDoc -> !Text
[ldMetricsDoc] :: LogDoc -> !Map Text Text
[ldNamespace] :: LogDoc -> ![([Text], [Text])]
[ldSeverityCoded] :: LogDoc -> !Maybe SeverityS
[ldPrivacyCoded] :: LogDoc -> !Maybe Privacy
[ldDetailsCoded] :: LogDoc -> !Maybe DetailLevel
[ldDetails] :: LogDoc -> ![DetailLevel]
[ldBackends] :: LogDoc -> ![BackendConfig]
[ldFiltered] :: LogDoc -> ![SeverityF]
[ldLimiter] :: LogDoc -> ![(Text, Double)]
[ldSilent] :: LogDoc -> Bool

data BackendConfig
Forwarder :: BackendConfig
Stdout :: FormatLogging -> BackendConfig
EKGBackend :: BackendConfig
DatapointBackend :: BackendConfig
PrometheusSimple :: Bool -> Maybe HostName -> PortNumber -> BackendConfig

-- | Type for the function foldTraceM from module
--   Cardano<i>Logging</i>Trace
newtype Folding a b
Folding :: b -> Folding a b

-- | Used as interface object for ForwarderTracer
data TraceObject
TraceObject :: !Maybe Text -> !Text -> ![Text] -> !SeverityS -> !DetailLevel -> !UTCTime -> !Text -> !Text -> TraceObject
[toHuman] :: TraceObject -> !Maybe Text
[toMachine] :: TraceObject -> !Text
[toNamespace] :: TraceObject -> ![Text]
[toSeverity] :: TraceObject -> !SeverityS
[toDetails] :: TraceObject -> !DetailLevel
[toTimestamp] :: TraceObject -> !UTCTime
[toHostname] :: TraceObject -> !Text
[toThreadId] :: TraceObject -> !Text
data PreFormatted
PreFormatted :: !UTCTime -> !Text -> !Text -> !Maybe Text -> Object -> PreFormatted
[pfTime] :: PreFormatted -> !UTCTime
[pfNamespace] :: PreFormatted -> !Text
[pfThreadId] :: PreFormatted -> !Text
[pfForHuman] :: PreFormatted -> !Maybe Text
[pfForMachineObject] :: PreFormatted -> Object
data HowToConnect

-- | Local pipe (UNIX or Windows).
LocalPipe :: !FilePath -> HowToConnect

-- | Remote socket (host and port).
RemoteSocket :: !Host -> !Port -> HowToConnect

-- | Adds a message object to a trace
traceWith :: Monad m => Trace m a -> a -> m ()
unfold :: Folding a b -> b

-- | Inverse of <a>use</a>.
arrow :: TracerA m a () -> Tracer m a
emptyConfigReflection :: IO ConfigReflection

-- | Call this function at initialisation, and later for reconfiguration.
--   Config reflection is used to optimise the tracers and has to collect
--   information about the tracers. Although it is possible to give more
--   then one tracer of the same time, it is not a common case to do this.
configureTracers :: forall a m. (MetaTrace a, MonadIO m) => ConfigReflection -> TraceConfig -> [Trace m a] -> m ()

-- | Take a selector function called <tt>extract</tt>. Take a function from
--   trace to trace with this config dependent value. In this way construct
--   a trace transformer with a config value
withNamespaceConfig :: forall m a b c. (MonadIO m, Ord b) => String -> (TraceConfig -> Namespace a -> m b) -> (Maybe b -> Trace m c -> m (Trace m a)) -> Trace m c -> m (Trace m a)

-- | Filter a trace by severity and take the filter value from the config
filterSeverityFromConfig :: MonadIO m => Trace m a -> m (Trace m a)

-- | Set detail level of a trace from the config
withDetailsFromConfig :: MonadIO m => Trace m a -> m (Trace m a)

-- | Routing and formatting of a trace from the config
withBackendsFromConfig :: MonadIO m => (Maybe [BackendConfig] -> Trace m FormattedMessage -> m (Trace m a)) -> m (Trace m a)

-- | Routing and formatting of a trace from the config
withLimitersFromConfig :: forall a m. MonadUnliftIO m => Trace m TraceDispatcherMessage -> Trace m a -> m (Trace m a)

-- | Switch off any message of a particular tracer based on the
--   configuration. If the top tracer is silent and no subtracer is not
--   silent, then switch it off
maybeSilent :: forall m a. MonadIO m => (TraceConfig -> Namespace a -> Bool) -> [Text] -> Bool -> Trace m a -> m (Trace m a)
isSilentTracer :: forall a. MetaTrace a => TraceConfig -> Namespace a -> Bool
hasNoMetrics :: forall a. MetaTrace a => TraceConfig -> Namespace a -> Bool

-- | If no severity can be found in the config, it is set to Warning
getSeverity :: TraceConfig -> Namespace a -> SeverityF

-- | If no details can be found in the config, it is set to DNormal
getDetails :: TraceConfig -> Namespace a -> DetailLevel

-- | If no backends can be found in the config, it is set to [EKGBackend,
--   Forwarder, Stdout HumanFormatColoured]
getBackends :: TraceConfig -> Namespace a -> [BackendConfig]

-- | Callback for doc collection
addFiltered :: MonadIO m => TraceControl -> Maybe SeverityF -> m ()

-- | Callback for doc collection
addLimiter :: MonadIO m => TraceControl -> (Text, Double) -> m ()
addSilent :: MonadIO m => TraceControl -> Maybe Bool -> m ()

-- | Limits the frequency of messages to nMsg which is given per minute.
--   
--   If the limiter detects more messages, it traces randomly selected
--   messages with the given frequency on the <tt>vtracer</tt> until the
--   frequency falls under the threshold long enough.(see below)
--   
--   Before this the <tt>ltracer</tt> gets a <a>StartLimiting</a> message.
--   In-between you receive <tt>ContinueLimiting</tt> messages on the
--   <tt>ltracer</tt> every <a>reminderPeriod</a> seconds, with the number
--   of suppressed messages. Finally it sends a <a>StopLimiting</a> message
--   on the <tt>ltracer</tt> and traces all messages on the
--   <tt>vtracer</tt> again.
--   
--   A budget is used to decide when to start limiting and stop limiting,
--   so that the limiter does not get activated if few messages are send in
--   high frequency, and doesn't get deactivated if their are only few
--   messages which come with low frequency. When messages arrive in
--   shorter frequency then by the given <tt>thresholdFrequency</tt> budget
--   is earned, and if they arrive in a longer period budget is spend. If
--   budget is gets higher then <a>budgetLimit</a>, the limiter starts, and
--   if it falls below minus <a>budgetLimit</a> the limiter stops.
limitFrequency :: forall a m. MonadUnliftIO m => Double -> Text -> Trace m TraceDispatcherMessage -> Trace m a -> m (Trace m a)

-- | Read a configuration file and returns the internal representation
readConfiguration :: FilePath -> IO TraceConfig

-- | Read a configuration file and returns the internal representation Uses
--   values which are not in the file from the defaultConfig
readConfigurationWithDefault :: FilePath -> TraceConfig -> IO TraceConfig

-- | Convert from internal to external representation
configToRepresentation :: TraceConfig -> ConfigRepresentation

-- | Checks if all namespaces in this configuration are legal. Legal in
--   this case means that it can be found by a hierarchical lookup in all
--   namespaces. Warns if namespaces in all namespaces are not unique,
--   Warns if namespaces in all namespaces are ending in the middle of
--   another namespace. The namespaces in allNamespaces are consistent with
--   the namespaces for the severityFor, privacyFor, detailsFor,
--   documentFor and metricsDocFor functions.
checkTraceConfiguration :: FilePath -> TraceConfig -> [([Text], [Text])] -> IO NSWarnings
checkTraceConfiguration' :: TraceConfig -> [([Text], [Text])] -> NSWarnings
documentTracer :: forall a. MetaTrace a => Trace IO a -> IO DocTracer
documentTracer' :: forall a a1. MetaTrace a => (Trace IO a1 -> IO (Trace IO a)) -> Trace IO a1 -> IO DocTracer
docuResultsToText :: DocTracer -> TraceConfig -> Text
docuResultsToMetricsHelptext :: DocTracer -> Text
docTracer :: MonadIO m => BackendConfig -> Trace m FormattedMessage
docTracerDatapoint :: MonadIO m => BackendConfig -> Trace m a

-- | Callback for doc collection
docIt :: MonadIO m => BackendConfig -> (LoggingContext, Either TraceControl a) -> m ()

-- | Convenience function for adding a namespace prefix to a documented
addDocumentedNamespace :: [Text] -> Documented a -> Documented a

-- | Format this trace as metrics
metricsFormatter :: forall a m. (LogFormatting a, MonadIO m) => Trace m FormattedMessage -> Trace m a

-- | Transform this trace to a preformatted message, so that double
--   serialization is avoided
preFormatted :: (LogFormatting a, MonadIO m) => Bool -> Trace m PreFormatted -> m (Trace m a)
forwardFormatter :: forall a m. MonadIO m => LogFormatting a => Trace m FormattedMessage -> m (Trace m a)

-- | Format this trace as TraceObject for the trace forwarder
forwardFormatter' :: forall m. MonadIO m => Trace m FormattedMessage -> Trace m PreFormatted
machineFormatter :: forall a m. (MonadIO m, LogFormatting a) => Trace m FormattedMessage -> m (Trace m a)

-- | Format this trace as TraceObject for machine-readable text output
--   (JSON)
machineFormatter' :: forall m. MonadIO m => Trace m FormattedMessage -> Trace m PreFormatted
cborFormatter :: forall a m. (MonadIO m, LogFormatting a) => Trace m FormattedMessage -> m (Trace m a)

-- | Format this trace in binary serialisation (CBOR)
cborFormatter' :: forall m. MonadIO m => Trace m FormattedMessage -> Trace m PreFormatted
humanFormatter :: forall a m. MonadIO m => LogFormatting a => Bool -> Trace m FormattedMessage -> m (Trace m a)

-- | Format this trace in human readable text output
humanFormatter' :: forall m. MonadIO m => Bool -> Trace m FormattedMessage -> Trace m PreFormatted

-- | Contramap a monadic function over a trace
contramapM :: Monad m => Trace m b -> ((LoggingContext, Either TraceControl a) -> m (LoggingContext, Either TraceControl b)) -> m (Trace m a)
threadLabelMe :: String -> IO ()

-- | Run an IO action which may throw an exception in a loop. On exception,
--   the action will be re-run after a pause. That pause doubles which each
--   exception, but is reset when the action runs long enough.
runInLoop :: IO () -> (SomeException -> IO ()) -> Word -> Word -> IO ()

-- | Convenience function for a Show instance to be converted to text
--   immediately
showT :: Show a => a -> Text

-- | Sets a new logging context for this message
withLoggingContext :: Monad m => LoggingContext -> Trace m a -> Trace m a
filterTrace :: Monad m => ((LoggingContext, a) -> Bool) -> Trace m a -> Trace m a
filterTraceMaybe :: Monad m => Trace m a -> Trace m (Maybe a)
filterTraceBySeverity :: Monad m => Maybe SeverityF -> Trace m a -> Trace m a
filterTraceByPrivacy :: Monad m => Maybe Privacy -> Trace m a -> Trace m a

-- | Sets severity for the messages in this trace
setSeverity :: Monad m => SeverityS -> Trace m a -> Trace m a

-- | Sets severities for the messages in this trace based on the MetaTrace
--   class
withSeverity :: forall m a. (Monad m, MetaTrace a) => Trace m a -> Trace m a

-- | Sets privacy Confidential for the messages in this trace
privately :: Monad m => Trace m a -> Trace m a

-- | Sets privacy for the messages in this trace
setPrivacy :: Monad m => Privacy -> Trace m a -> Trace m a

-- | Sets privacy for the messages in this trace based on the MetaTrace
--   class
withPrivacy :: forall m a. (Monad m, MetaTrace a) => Trace m a -> Trace m a
allPublic :: a -> Privacy
allConfidential :: a -> Privacy

-- | Sets detail level for the messages in this trace
setDetails :: Monad m => DetailLevel -> Trace m a -> Trace m a

-- | Sets detail level for the messages in this trace based on the message
withDetails :: forall m a. (Monad m, MetaTrace a) => Trace m a -> Trace m a

-- | Contramap a monadic function over a trace Can as well filter out
--   messages
contramapMCond :: Monad m => Trace m b -> ((LoggingContext, Either TraceControl a) -> m (Maybe (LoggingContext, Either TraceControl b))) -> m (Trace m a)
contramapM' :: Monad m => ((LoggingContext, Either TraceControl a) -> m ()) -> Trace m a

-- | Folds the monadic cata function with acc over a. Uses an MVar to store
--   the state
foldTraceM :: forall a acc m. MonadUnliftIO m => (acc -> LoggingContext -> a -> m acc) -> acc -> Trace m (Folding a acc) -> m (Trace m a)

-- | Like foldTraceM, but filter the trace by a predicate.
foldCondTraceM :: forall a acc m. MonadUnliftIO m => (acc -> LoggingContext -> a -> m acc) -> acc -> (a -> Bool) -> Trace m (Folding a acc) -> m (Trace m a)

-- | Allows to route to different tracers, based on the message being
--   processed. The second argument must mappend all possible tracers of
--   the first argument to one tracer. This is required for the
--   configuration!
routingTrace :: forall m a. Monad m => (a -> m (Trace m a)) -> Trace m a -> Trace m a

-- | Sets names for the messages in this trace based on the selector
--   function and appends the provided names to the context.
withNames :: forall m a. (Monad m, MetaTrace a) => [Text] -> Trace m a -> Trace m a

-- | Appends a name to the context. E.g. appendName "specific" $ appendName
--   "middle" $ appendName "general" tracer give the result:
--   `general.middle.specific`.
appendPrefixName :: Monad m => Text -> Trace m a -> Trace m a

-- | Appends all names to the context.
appendPrefixNames :: Monad m => [Text] -> Trace m a -> Trace m a
appendInnerName :: Monad m => Text -> Trace m a -> Trace m a

-- | Appends all names to the context.
appendInnerNames :: Monad m => [Text] -> Trace m a -> Trace m a

-- | Sets names for the messages in this trace based on the selector
--   function
withInnerNames :: forall m a. (Monad m, MetaTrace a) => Trace m a -> Trace m a

-- | A contramap' which is strict in its second argument and its result
--   captures a common pattern to avoid unintentionally leaking space when
--   composing tracers. The infix alias is (&gt;!$!&lt;).
contramap' :: Contravariant f => (a' -> a) -> f a -> f a'

-- | A contramap' which is strict in its second argument and its result
--   captures a common pattern to avoid unintentionally leaking space when
--   composing tracers. The infix alias is (&gt;!$!&lt;).
(>!$!<) :: Contravariant f => (a' -> a) -> f a -> f a'
infixl 4 >!$!<
initDataPointStore :: IO DataPointStore

-- | Write <a>DataPoint</a> to the store.
writeToStore :: DataPointStore -> DataPointName -> DataPoint -> IO ()
dataPointTracer :: forall m. MonadIO m => DataPointStore -> Trace m DataPoint
mkDataPointTracer :: forall dp. (ToJSON dp, MetaTrace dp, NFData dp) => Trace IO DataPoint -> IO (Trace IO dp)

-- | It is mandatory to construct only one standard tracer in any
--   application! Throwing away a standard tracer and using a new one will
--   result in an exception
ekgTracer :: MonadIO m => TraceConfig -> Store -> m (Trace m FormattedMessage)
showTReal :: RealFloat a => a -> Text

-- | The standardTracer handles stdout logging in a thread-safe manner. It
--   is strongly advised to construct only one standardTracer for any
--   application.
standardTracer :: forall m. MonadIO m => m (Trace m FormattedMessage)

-- | It is mandatory to construct only one forwardTracer tracer in any
--   application! Throwing away a forwardTracer tracer and using a new one
--   will result in an exception
forwardTracer :: forall m. MonadIO m => (TraceObject -> IO ()) -> Trace m FormattedMessage

-- | Construct a tracer according to the requirements for cardano node. The
--   tracer gets a <tt>name</tt>, which is appended to its namespace. The
--   tracer has to be an instance of LogFormatting for the display of
--   messages and an instance of MetaTrace for meta information such as
--   severity, privacy, details and backends'. The tracer gets the
--   backends': <tt>trStdout</tt>, <tt>trForward</tt> and <tt>mbTrEkg</tt>
--   as arguments. The returned tracer needs to be configured with a
--   configuration before it is used.
mkCardanoTracer :: forall evt. (LogFormatting evt, MetaTrace evt) => Trace IO FormattedMessage -> Trace IO FormattedMessage -> Maybe (Trace IO FormattedMessage) -> [Text] -> IO (Trace IO evt)

-- | Adds the possibility to add special tracers via the hook function
mkCardanoTracer' :: forall evt evt1. (LogFormatting evt1, MetaTrace evt1) => Trace IO FormattedMessage -> Trace IO FormattedMessage -> Maybe (Trace IO FormattedMessage) -> [Text] -> (Trace IO evt1 -> IO (Trace IO evt)) -> IO (Trace IO evt)
mkMetricsTracer :: Maybe (Trace IO FormattedMessage) -> Trace IO FormattedMessage
traceTracerInfo :: Trace IO FormattedMessage -> Trace IO FormattedMessage -> ConfigReflection -> IO ()
traceConfigWarnings :: Trace IO FormattedMessage -> Trace IO FormattedMessage -> [Text] -> IO ()
traceEffectiveConfiguration :: Trace IO FormattedMessage -> Trace IO FormattedMessage -> TraceConfig -> IO ()
getMetricName :: Metric -> Text

-- | A helper function for creating an empty |Object|.
emptyObject :: HashMap Text a
emptyLoggingContext :: LoggingContext
nsReplacePrefix :: [Text] -> Namespace a -> Namespace a
nsReplaceInner :: [Text] -> Namespace a -> Namespace a
nsCast :: Namespace a -> Namespace b
nsPrependInner :: Text -> Namespace a -> Namespace b
nsGetComplete :: Namespace a -> [Text]
nsGetTuple :: Namespace a -> ([Text], [Text])
nsRawToText :: ([Text], [Text]) -> Text
nsToText :: Namespace a -> Text
defaultForwarder :: TraceOptionForwarder
emptyTraceConfig :: TraceConfig
emptyLogDoc :: Text -> [(Text, Text)] -> LogDoc

-- | Do an emitting effect. Contrast with <a>effect</a> which does not make
--   the tracer an emitting tracer.
emit :: Applicative m => (a -> m ()) -> TracerA m a ()

-- | Do a non-emitting effect. This effect will only be run if some part of
--   the tracer downstream emits (see <a>emit</a>).
effect :: (a -> m b) -> TracerA m a b

-- | Ignore the input and do not emit. The name is intended to lead to
--   clear and suggestive arrow expressions.
squelch :: Applicative m => TracerA m a ()

-- | Use a natural transformation to change the underlying monad.
nat :: (forall x. m x -> n x) -> TracerA m a b -> TracerA n a b

-- | Inverse of <a>arrow</a>. Useful when writing arrow tracers which use a
--   contravariant tracer (the newtype in this module).
use :: Tracer m a -> TracerA m a ()

-- | Trace strings to stdout. Output could be jumbled when this is used
--   from multiple threads. Consider <a>debugTracer</a> instead.
stdoutTracer :: Tracer IO String

-- | Trace strings using <a>traceM</a>. This will use stderr. See
--   documentation in <a>Debug.Trace</a> for more details.
debugTracer :: Applicative m => Tracer m String

-- | Use a natural transformation to change the <tt>m</tt> type. This is
--   useful, for instance, to use concrete IO tracers in monad transformer
--   stacks that have IO as their base.
natTracer :: forall m n s. (forall x. m x -> n x) -> Tracer m s -> Tracer n s

-- | Run a tracer only for the Just variant of a Maybe. If it's Nothing,
--   the <a>nullTracer</a> is used (no output).
--   
--   The arrow representation allows for proper laziness: if the tracer
--   parameter does not produce any tracing effects, then the predicate
--   won't even be evaluated. Contrast with the simple contravariant
--   representation as <tt>a -&gt; m ()</tt>, in which the predicate _must_
--   be forced no matter what, because it's impossible to know a priori
--   whether that function will not produce any tracing effects.
--   
--   It's written out explicitly for demonstration. Could also use arrow
--   notation:
--   
--   <pre>
--   traceMaybe p tr = Tracer $ proc a -&gt; do
--     case k a of
--       Just b  -&gt; use tr        -&lt; b
--       Nothing -&gt; Arrow.squelch -&lt; ()
--   </pre>
traceMaybe :: Monad m => (a -> Maybe b) -> Tracer m b -> Tracer m a

-- | Uses <a>traceMaybe</a> to give a tracer which emits only if a
--   predicate is true.
squelchUnless :: Monad m => (a -> Bool) -> Tracer m a -> Tracer m a
showTHex :: Integral a => a -> Text
runTracer :: Tracer m a -> TracerA m a ()
